{{Short description|German experimental psychologist and professor}}
'''Katharina A. Schwarz''' (*born 1985 in [[Würzburg]]) is a German experimental psychologist and professor of social psychology at the [[University of Luxembourg]].

She studied biology at the [[University of Würzburg|Julius Maximilian University of Würzburg]] and then pursued a doctorate in Systems Neuroscience at the [[University Medical Center Hamburg-Eppendorf]] from 2011 to 2015. From 2015 to 2024 she worked as a research associate and project leader at the Department of Psychology at the [[University of Würzburg]]. Research stays included visits to the [[Massachusetts Institute of Technology]] and the [[University of Canterbury]] in Christchurch. In 2024 she joined the Department of Psychology at [[University of Trier|Trier University]], and in November 2025 she was appointed professor of social psychology (Associate Professor of Social Psychology) at the [[University of Luxembourg]].

Her research focuses on action in social contexts, perceived control, decision-making, sustainable behavior, and expectations. She is a partner in the EU-HORIZON project ''BUTTERFLY''<ref>{{Cite web |last=Thijs |first=Simon |date=2025-02-26 |title=Hummel, Schmetterling & Co wieder stärken |url=https://idw-online.de/de/news848049 |access-date=2025-09-30 |website=Informationsdienst Wissenschaft (idw)}}</ref> and a long-standing advocate of the [[open science]] movement.

== Awards ==
* 2018: Preregistration prize awarded by the Center for Open Science<ref>{{Cite web |last=Science |first=Center for Open |title=December 2018 winners of the Prereg Prize |url=https://www.cos.io/about/news/december-2018-prereg-winners |access-date=2025-09-30 |website=www.cos.io |language=en}}</ref>
* 2020: Karl Marbe Prize of the Institute of Psychology at the University of Würzburg<ref>{{Cite web |title=Karl-Marbe-Preis - Institut für Psychologie |url=https://www.psychologie.uni-wuerzburg.de/forschung/forschungspreise/karl-marbe-preis/ |access-date=2025-09-30 |website=www.psychologie.uni-wuerzburg.de |language=de-DE}}</ref>
* 2022: ZONTA Science Award 2022 of the Zonta Club Würzburg<ref>{{Cite web |title=ZONTA-Preis für Wissenschaftlerin der JMU |url=https://www.uni-wuerzburg.de/aktuelles/einblick/single/news/zonta-preis-fuer-wissenschaftlerin-der-jmu-1/ |access-date=2025-09-30 |website=www.uni-wuerzburg.de |language=de-DE}}</ref>

== Publications ==
* Schwarz, K. A., Pfister, R. & Büchel, C. (2016). Rethinking Explicit Expectations: Connecting Placebos, Social Cognition, and Contextual Perception. ''Trends in Cognitive Sciences, 20(6), 469–480.''<ref>{{Cite web |title=Psychology: The power of expectations |url=https://idw-online.de/de/news651278 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160514103955/https://idw-online.de/de/news651278 |archive-date=2016-05-14 |access-date=2025-09-30 |website=idw-online.de |language=de}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Schwarz |first1=Katharina A. |last2=Pfister |first2=Roland |last3=Büchel |first3=Christian |date=2016-06-01 |title=Rethinking Explicit Expectations: Connecting Placebos, Social Cognition, and Contextual Perception |url=https://www.cell.com/trends/cognitive-sciences/abstract/S1364-6613(16)30008-0 |journal=Trends in Cognitive Sciences |language=English |volume=20 |issue=6 |pages=469–480 |doi=10.1016/j.tics.2016.04.001 |issn=1364-6613 |pmid=27108268|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
* Schwarz, K. A., Pfister, R., Kluge, M., Weller, L., & Kunde, W. (2018). Do we see it or not? Sensory attenuation in the visual domain. ''Journal of Experimental Psychology: General, 147''(3), 418-430.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Schwarz |first1=Katharina A. |last2=Pfister |first2=Roland |last3=Kluge |first3=Michel |last4=Weller |first4=Lisa |last5=Kunde |first5=Wilfried |date=2018-03-01 |title=Do we see it or not? Sensory attenuation in the visual domain. |url=https://doi.apa.org/doi/10.1037/xge0000353 |journal=Journal of Experimental Psychology: General |language=en |volume=147 |issue=3 |pages=418–430 |doi=10.1037/xge0000353 |pmid=29154616 |issn=1939-2222|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
* Pfister, R.*, Tonn, S.*, Weller, L., Kunde, W., & Schwarz, K. A. (2021). To prevent means to know: Explicit but no implicit agency for prevention behavior. ''Cognition'', ''206'', 104489. (* equal author contribution)<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Pfister |first1=Roland |last2=Tonn |first2=Solveig |last3=Weller |first3=Lisa |last4=Kunde |first4=Wilfried |last5=Schwarz |first5=Katharina A. |date=2021-01-01 |title=To prevent means to know: Explicit but no implicit agency for prevention behavior |url=https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0010027720303085 |journal=Cognition |volume=206 |issue=1 |article-number=104489 |doi=10.1016/j.cognition.2020.104489 |pmid=33254006 |issn=0010-0277|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
* Schwarz, K. A., Klaffehn, A. L., Hauke-Forman, N., Muth, F. V., & Pfister, R. (2022). Never run a changing system: Action-effect contingency shapes prospective agency. ''Cognition'', ''229'', 105250.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Schwarz |first1=Katharina A. |last2=Klaffehn |first2=Annika L. |last3=Hauke-Forman |first3=Nicole |last4=Muth |first4=Felicitas V. |last5=Pfister |first5=Roland |date=2022-12-01 |title=Never run a changing system: Action-effect contingency shapes prospective agency |url=https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0010027722002384 |journal=Cognition |volume=229 |issue=1 |article-number=105250 |doi=10.1016/j.cognition.2022.105250 |pmid=35963118 |issn=0010-0277|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
* Schwarz, K. A., Tonn, S., Büttner, J., Kunde, W., & Pfister, R. (2023). Sense of agency in social hierarchies. ''Journal of Experimental Psychology: General, 152''(10), 2957-2976''.''<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Schwarz |first1=Katharina A. |last2=Tonn |first2=Solveig |last3=Büttner |first3=Johannes |last4=Kunde |first4=Wilfried |last5=Pfister |first5=Roland |date=2023-10-01 |title=Sense of agency in social hierarchies. |url=https://doi.apa.org/doi/10.1037/xge0001426 |journal=Journal of Experimental Psychology: General |language=en |volume=152 |issue=10 |pages=2957–2976 |doi=10.1037/xge0001426 |pmid=37166843 |issn=1939-2222|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
* Schwarz, K. A. (2025). Perceived control and the pleasantness of choosing: How much choice is too much choice? ''Current Research in Behavioral Sciences'', 8, 100174.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Schwarz |first=Katharina A. |date=2025-01-01 |title=Perceived control and the pleasantness of choosing: How much choice is too much choice? |url=https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2666518225000075 |journal=Current Research in Behavioral Sciences |volume=8 |article-number=100174 |doi=10.1016/j.crbeha.2025.100174 |issn=2666-5182|doi-access=free }}</ref>
